Benevolence
To be a Christian is to acknowledge that you are needy. It’s one of the marks we read about in the Sermon on the Mount: “Blessed are the poor in spirit for theirs is the kingdom of heaven” (Matt. 5:3). To follow Jesus, you must acknowledge you are spiritually bankrupt and incapable of saving yourself.
A church, then, is a gathering of needy people. Every member recognizes his or her need for the righteousness of Christ (Rom. 3:20), for God’s grace (Eph. 2:5), and for forgiveness (Col. 1:13-14).
That said, our physical needs will vary. We may be on the same sea, but we’re not in the same boat. Some of our boats have holes, some lack paddles. Kaleo church’s benevolence ministry begins here: showing each other mercy amidst our different physical needs. In other words, benevolence is not only about giving out money to help solve problems. It’s about showing others the mercy and love we’ve been shown in Christ.
If you call Kaleo Church home and find yourself in physical need beyond what you are able to provide, please take some time to fill out this form. A church leader will set up a time to pray and consult with you about your situation. Part of the consultation will be looking at ways to prevent this situation from becoming long-term and exploring other strategies to provide for your physical needs.
Fill out the benevolence request form here

Camp Maranatha Info
Sunday Service at Camp Maranatha Info
Camp Address:
19780 W. Camp Dr,
Mile 1.5 Horseshoe Lake Rd.
Big Lake AK 99652
Driving Directions:
1. Parks Highway north to Big Lake Turnoff
2. Left on Big Lake Road (drive approx. 3 miles)
3. Right on Beaver Lake Road (drive approx. 1 mile)
4. Left on West Lakes Blvd (drive approx. 3.5 miles)
5. Right on 2nd Horseshoe Lake Road (drive approx. 2 miles)
6. Left at Camp Lane (1/4 mile)
Sunday Service Info:
Kaleo's Sunday Service on July 9th, is at 10:45am (Yes, an hour earlier than usual) at Camp Maranatha in the Gym.
A buffet style lunch will be provided by the camp in the chow hall after the service for everyone. Join us for lunch after the service EVEN IF YOU DIDN'T SIGN UP to be at the retreat, and feel free to stay till 5pm and hang out and have fun at the lake or play games.
Click Here for a map of the Camp

Chris Cha
Lead Pastor and Elder
Chris was born and raised in Honolulu, Hawaii. He moved to Anchorage at the start of his junior year and graduated from East High School (go T-birds!).
Chris married the love of his life, Anna, in September of 1999 and now have six wonderful children. Chris graduated from Dallas Christian College with a degree in Ministry & Leadership, and is completing a master’s degree in Global Leadership at Western Seminary.
He loves spending time with his wife and kids, watching movies, eating good food, playing board games, playing and watching basketball, and reading. His life verse is John 3:30 , “He must increase, but I must decrease.” Chris is most thankful for the abounding love of the Father, the amazing grace of Jesus Christ, the gracious fellowship of the Holy Spirit, Anna Cha, each of his children, and the Kaleo family.

App Setup Instructions
Two Ways to Use Church Center: Website and App
- Access via website: https://kaleoalaska.churchcenter.com/home
- Download the App: Available free for Apple and Android. See Links below.
We love the Church Center App and all that you can do through it:
- Check-in your children in on Sunday mornings
- Manage your giving
- Register for events
- Access your K-Group or browse and sign up for a K-Group
- Access our members directory
It puts the things you need right in the palm of your hand. Watch Video
Download & Setup the App:
Simply download the Church Center App from your App Store (available free for Apple and Android) and follow the instructions to allow Church Center to use your location to find the Kaleo Church. Choose Kaleo Church, click “This is my church.” Finish the prompts to log in for the first time and select your profile. If you don’t already have a profile in our system, follow the prompts to create one.

Cody Carpenter
Associate Pastor & Elder
Cody was born and raised in Anchorage and graduated from Service High School. He met his wife while earning a B.S. in Math, Science and Aviation at Central Christian College of Kansas. They were married in 2001.
A year after marriage, Cody brought Sara back to Anchorage with him where they began fostering children and eventually adopting from foster care. Through a combination of fostering, adopting and natural born, they have 9 children. He has worked in various industries including construction and engineering.
If not at the church office, you can expect Cody to be found renovating some part of his house, wood-working in his garage, or hunting and fishing. Cody was among the Kaleo church plant launch team from Cornerstone Church and has served first as an elder and Associate Pastor since 2016.

Darron Rizer
Elder
Darron was born and raised in Anchorage and graduated from Service High School. He met his wife, Jennifer, while earning a B.A. in Business Management at Abilene Christian University in Texas. Post-grad, he completed a Master’s of Science in Financial Services (MSFS). Jenny and Darron were married in Arlington, Texas, December 2000.
Six months after marriage, Darron brought Jenny back to Anchorage with him where they planted roots and began a family. God blessed them with four children. He has also been blessed in his career as a financial advisor since graduating college.
Darron looks forward to spending quality time with his family whenever possible. He is active at church with his continued involvement Kaleo Men’s Ministry. During his free time, He loves spending time in the great outdoors hunting, fishing, hiking, boating and camping. Darron was among the Kaleo church plant launch team from Cornerstone Church and has served in K-group and men’s ministry from the beginning.

Give
"Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver." ~2 Corinthians 9:7
SUNDAYS OFFERING BOXES
We are not under compulsion to give. Rather, we should cheerfully offer what God has placed on our hearts. We consider giving to be a form of worship to the Lord, which is why we receive these offerings during our Sunday gatherings via the offering boxes in the back of the chapel.
ONLINE GIVING
We also offer an online giving option. To give securely online, click HERE .
GIVE BY MAIL
If you would like to mail your donation, please send it to:
Kaleo Church
1301 W 100th Ave,
Anchorage, AK 99515

Jay Ubben
Elder
Jay grew up in Gig Harbor WA and in 2013 moved to Alaska. He is a project manager for a construction company building infrastructure in the most remote parts of the state. Jay enjoys every outdoor activity Alaska has to offer.
Jay married his incredible wife, Caylee, in 2016 and started attending Kaleo shortly after. They have three wonderful kids and have been led to foster teen boys.
His passion is to intentionally walk with others in community and do life together. The verse he lives by is Proverbs 27:17 "As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another." He strives to keep things simple and love God and love others.

Job Description: Assistant Sunday School Teacher
Opportunity: Kaleo Kids Assistant Sunday School Teacher
Summary:
Kaleo Kids is a thriving ministry for children centered around the good news of Jesus. Kids learn that God made them, He is the rescuer from sin and death, and He will make all things new. Curriculum comes from the Gospel Project. Every lesson comes back to a Christ centered connection. We see Jesus from Genesis to Revelation. Kids play games, do crafts and activities, laugh and have fun together.
Time Requirements:
10:45 a.m. – 1:30 p.m. One Sunday a month for nursery, toddlers, and PreK. Two Sunday’s every 6 weeks on a rotational schedule for K-5 th classes.
This Position Reports to:
Children’s Ministry Director, Lucy Matthew, lmatthew@kaleoalaska.org.
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Assist lead teacher with classroom management, activities and teaching as needed.
- Check-in with your lead teacher on Saturday night to confirm for Sunday morning.
- Attend 10:45 a.m. devotion/communion on Sunday Mornings that you are scheduled to serve, led by Pastor Chris for church wide ministry team of volunteers for that Sunday.
- Access Ministry Grid for Online lesson plans and dates.
- Accept scheduling confirmation on Planning Center for Kaleo Church.
- Attend quarterly children’s meeting for one hour after church, (4 times a year.)
Qualifications:
- Member of Kaleo Church
- Enjoy working with and caring for children (Nursery – 5 th )
- Pass basic background check through Department of Justice, paid for by Kaleo.
Ready to Apply?

Job Description: Audio Tech
Opportunity: Audio Tech
Summary:
The Worship Arts ministry at Kaleo is actively engaged in preparing hearts for worship through song. Every team member plays an active role in leading others in worship and removing obstacles. The A/V tech team plays a vital role in making the worship team heard, visual aids seen, and our Sunday service available online.
Time Requirements:
- Serve one Sunday service a month plus the prior Tuesday rehearsal.
- Tuesday evenings prior to the Sunday that you are serving typically from 7pm-9pm (2 hour time window may vary depending on team availability).
- Sundays: 10:45am (team prayer) -1:30pm when scheduled that week.
This Position Reports to:
Worship Arts Director, Mary Giles mgiles@kaleoalaska.org
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Connect band and vocalists to sound board at rehearsal and services. Adjust audio settings on sound desk to mix proper volume, EQ and monitor levels as needed.
- At Tuesday rehearsal:
- Dial in a 90% mix while band is rehearsing and save the scene to the sound board for use on Sunday morning.
- Coordinate with the band to record each of the songs for the band to practice to during the week. Distribute the recordings as directed by the WA Director.
- Help new members of the band understand how to adjust the monitor levels.
- At Sunday Services:
- Replace the batteries in all wireless mics and any instruments that require a battery.
- After Service has finished:
- Properly shut down and put away all audio equipment.
- Turn off all lighting (stage, overhead, string lights, and any other decorative lights).
Qualifications:
- No experience necessary, but must be comfortable with technology and willing to learn.
- A criminal background check will be done. A flagged background will not necessarily disqualify you from this role, but it would need to be discussed.
Ready to apply?

Job Description: Bass Player
Opportunity: Bass Guitar Player
Summary:
Worship is any discipline that acknowledges God as worthy of our lives (i.e. time and energy). It is a physical practice toward spiritual wellbeing. At Kaleo, we worship on Sunday mornings through singing traditional and contemporary songs as a congregation. These songs are arranged for and played by a contemporary band of vocalists, keyboard, electric and acoustic guitar, bass, and drums. The bass guitar bridges the rhythm section to the melodic instruments in a contemporary worship band. Bass guitar provides rich, low sound and gives character to the percussive beat of the drums to create a “groove” to a song.
Time Requirements:
- 2 hour evening practice the Tuesday before you serve
- 1+ hours of individual practice at home
- 5 hours (10:45am-1:15pm) at the Sunday service
- Serve a minimum of once every two months
This Position Reports to:
Worship Director, Mary Giles, mgiles@kaleoalaska.org
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Humility and a heart for serving others
- Consideration of how your performance is affecting congregant’s worship experience
- Partner with the drummer to establish and maintain a steady tempo
- Drive the energy of a song appropriately and according to the worship leader’s direction
- Help guide the group toward a correct groove if they get off
- Give useful feedback to the worship leader while always being willing to submit to the leader for a final say in song direction
Qualifications:
- Be able to play well with others
- Able to hear and lock rhythm with the drummer’s kick
- Be able to listen to a song and identify when and how to raise and lower the energy
- Ability to play in any key or time signature that classic and contemporary worship songs are played in
- Be creative and adaptable to the arrangement decided on by the worship director
- Complete a background check
Ready to apply?

Job Description: Drummer
Opportunity: Drummer
Summary:
Worship is any discipline that acknowledges God as worthy of our lives (i.e. time and energy). It is a physical practice toward spiritual wellbeing. At Kaleo, we worship on Sunday mornings through singing traditional and contemporary songs as a congregation. These songs are arranged for and played by a contemporary band of vocalists, keyboard, electric and acoustic guitar, bass, and drums. The drummer establishes and maintains tempo and drives the energy of the songs. They may play on a full drum set or smaller percussion instrument like a cajon.
Time Requirements:
- 2 hour evening practice the Tuesday before you serve
- 1+ hours of individual practice at home
- 5 hours (10:45am-1:15pm) at the Sunday service
- Serve a minimum of once every two months
This Position Reports to:
Worship Director, Mary Giles, mgiles@kaleoalaska.org
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Humility and a heart for serving others
- Consideration of how your performance is affecting congregant’s worship experience
- Establish and maintain a steady tempo
- Make artistic choices that aid the overall effect of the song
- Lead the group toward a correct tempo if they get off
- Give useful feedback to the worship leader while always being willing to submit to the leader for a final say in song direction
Qualifications:
- Be able to play well with others
- Understand and be able to play in at least cut time, 3/4, 4/4, and 6/8 time signatures
- Be able to listen to a song, and identify and play the tempo, basic beats, and fills
- Be creative and adaptable to the arrangement decided on by the worship director
- Complete a background check

Job Description: Hospitality Event Host
Opportunity: Hospitality - Event Host
Summary:
A big part of the gospel is hospitality. Hospitality is very important at Kaleo because it is often the “welcome” that a person needs to feel to know that they belong. One of the key ways we want to be hospitable at Kaleo is through food at our many gatherings and events. Some of these gatherings are regular monthly events (ie: Greet n Grub) while others may be less frequent (ie: Membership Class). An Event Host is in charge of making sure that a Kaleo event (that has food) has all of the necessary food items as well as taking care of the setup, teardown and cleanup of all of the food related parts of the event.
Time Requirements:
- Approximately 1-2 hours to shop and 2-4 hours/event that you sign up for. We encourage volunteers to be available for 1 event per month or every other month.
- Required trainings, as requested.
This Position Reports to:
Associate Pastor, Cody Carpenter, ccarpenter@kaleoalaska.org
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Determine the needs of the event from the ministry lead such as: Light snacks and beverages or full meal? How many attendees? Allergies of the attendees?
- Shop for food and other supplies as needed. This is normally best done at Costco but may make sense at other grocers as needed. Check inventory of supplies (Paper plates, utensils, condiments, cleaning supplies, etc.). Reimbursement options or carrying a church credit card are both options depending on frequency of serving.
- Prepare food or arrange catering (depends on event needs).
- Setup Food in event space. May mean setting up serving tables with table cloths at times.
- Serve the attendees with a smile and servant attitude.
- Clean-up. Help to take food back to kitchen and wash and put away any serving dishes and utensils that were used.
Qualifications:
- Background in food service, catering, cooking for big families, or just an overall enjoyment of food related service is needed.
- Enjoy working with food and serving others.
- Pass background check.
Ready to apply?

Job Description: K-group Leader
Opportunity: K-Group Leader
Summary:
Kaleo's K-Group ministry is the backbone of how our community is structured. Our people gather weekly in homes to fellowship, serve, learn and grow spiritually. A K-Group Leader is a single person or married couple willing to gather and shepherd a small group (Koinonia-Group, AKA K-Group) of congregants on a weekly basis.
Time Requirements:
- K-group leaders should plan on leading a once-weekly gathering in a home for 3+/- hours from September-May (K-group seasons are matched to the school year). For summer and December, we only ask that you gather your K-group once a month.
- The K-groups pastor will lead a topical training (typically with food and childcare provided) about 4 times a year (October, January, March, May) that will last about 2-3 hours.
- Twice a semester, the K-group pastor will meet with you (spouses encouraged to join) for a check-in. Normally this is done over lunch or dinner.
- Every August, we ask that our existing and new K-group leaders gather once a week for 3-weeks for about 2-3 hours each week to go through a training “boot camp” before kicking off a new season. Dinner and childcare provided.
This Position Reports to: K-Groups Director – Pastor Cody Carpenter
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Gathering your K-group weekly. Communication is key.
- Leading your K-Group by praying, teaching/learning the bible, being on mission, fellowshipping and eating together. Not every one of these elements will happen in a weekly gathering but they should be happening throughout the course of a ministry season.
- K-group leaders do not necessarily need to host the group in their own home, but it can be helpful if they are able to. Hosting can happen at another participant’s house or at the church if needed.
- Leaders will need to lead the group and help delegate duties to other members.
- Keep their K-group "Groups" page updated with pertinent information (attendance, vision for the group, communication with the group) and be responsive and warm when a new member requests to join.
Qualifications:
- Have a sincere and mature walk with the Lord.
- Be part of an existing K-group.
- Have your current K-group leader’s recommendation to lead.
- Be a Kaleo Church member in good standing.
- Possess a desire to lead and a willingness to submit to the Lord’s direction and to church leadership.
- Pass a background check. If there is a flag in your background, it does not necessarily disqualify you. We would just ask to have a conversation about it and take it case-by-case, dependent on the severity, time since offense, and the applicant's maturity.
Ready to Apply?

Job Description: Kaleo Kids Check-in Volunteer
Opportunity: Kaleo Kids Check-in Volunteer
Summary:
Kaleo Kids is a thriving ministry for children centered around the good news of Jesus. Kids learn that God made them, He is the rescuer from sin and death, and He will make all things new. In order for kids to come to class, they must check-in at the Welcome Table in the lobby. This is so important as it helps people feel welcome at Kaleo, but also keeps our children secure and safe.
Time Requirements:
10:45 – 1:30 Sunday mornings once or twice a week on a rotational schedule.
This Position Reports to:
Children’s Ministry Director, Lucy Matthew, lmatthew@kaleoalaska.org.
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Learn the set up and take down process. It’s easy.
- Be a friendly face to welcome folks in the morning at the Welcome Table.
- Direct parents and children to where Sunday school classes are located.
- Attend 10:45 a.m. devotion/communion on Sunday Mornings that you are scheduled to serve, led by Pastor Chris for church wide ministry team of volunteers for that Sunday.
- Accept scheduling confirmation on Planning Center for Kaleo Church.
- Attend quarterly children’s meeting for one hour after church, (4 times a year.)
Qualifications:
- Member of Kaleo Church
- Pass basic background check through Department of Justice, paid for by Kaleo.

Job Description: Kaleo Kids Teen Helper
Opportunity: Kaleo Kids Teen Helper
Summary:
Kaleo Kids is a thriving ministry for children centered around the good news of Jesus. Kids learn that God made them, He is the rescuer from sin and death, and He will make all things new. Curriculum comes from the Gospel Project. Every lesson comes back to a Christ centered connection. We see Jesus from Genesis to Revelation. Kids play games, do crafts and activities, laugh and have fun together.
Time Requirements:
10:45 a.m. – 1:30 p.m. One Sunday a month for nursery, toddlers, and PreK. Two Sunday’s every 6 weeks on a rotational schedule for K-5 th classes.
This Position Reports to:
Children’s Ministry Director, Lucy Matthew, lmatthew@kaleoalaska.org.
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Assist lead teacher as directed.
- Make children feel welcome, talk to them, and do activities with them.
- Encourage children to sit still and listen when needed.
- Check-in with your lead teacher on Saturday night to confirm for Sunday morning.
- If possible, attend devotion and communion at 10:45 am. Sunday mornings.
Qualifications:
- Love Jesus and be responsible.
- Enjoy working with and encouraging children.

Job Description: Kaleo Student Ministry (KSM) Leader
Opportunity: Kaleo Student Ministry (KSM) Leader
Summary:
KSM serves 6th-12th grade students. We love to hang out and have fun together while growing in our faith, understanding, and love for God and others. At KSM, we seek true wisdom for life and desire to experience safe, authentic community. KSM high school and middle school students meet on the 2nd, 3rd, and 4th Sundays of the month from 1:30-3 pm for lunch, teaching, games, and fellowship, typically at the church. We also gather to serve missionally through 5th Sunday Service Projects. Additionally, we offer weekly bible study opportunities in homes, retreats/camps, and special events throughout the year.
Time Requirements:
Approximately 2-3 hours/week for Sunday gatherings. Initial commitment is for September through May.
- KSM Leaders are expected to serve from 1:15 pm until all students have been picked up by parents.
- Additional support may be needed during special events. Leaders may also provide support at weekly bible studies and retreats/camps, as schedules allow.
- Required trainings, as requested.
This Position Reports to:
KSM Director, Kerri Howell, khowell@kaleoalaska.org
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Be attentive to students at all times, and invest relationally in all students.
- Confirm that students are checked in online and checked out by a parent or approved driver upon dismissal.
- Help maintain order during group times, and follow all safety, security, and risk management protocols.
- Direct students for room cleanup after meetings.
Qualifications:
- Enjoy working with and caring for pre-teens and teenagers.
- Must be 18 years of age; if 18, must have graduated from high school.
- Pass background check through Department of Justice.
Ready to apply?

Job Description: Online Streaming Tech
Opportunity: Online Streaming Tech
Summary:
The Worship Arts ministry at Kaleo is actively engaged in preparing hearts for worship through song. Every team member plays an active role in leading others in worship and removing obstacles. The A/V tech team plays a vital role in making the worship team heard, visual aids seen, and our Sunday service available online. The Online Streaming Technician sets up and runs the Vimeo and Facebook live streams of our Sunday services. This also provides the archival recording of each sermon.
Time Requirements:
- Sundays, 10:45am-1:30pm, at least once every two months
This Position Reports to:
Worship Arts Director, Mary Giles mgiles@kaleoalaska.org
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Using Vimeo, Facebook, OBS software and a video switcher; set up a live stream of the Sunday service
- Switch camera angels when appropriate
- Set up and maintain adequate sound quality
- Troubleshoot any issues before or during the service
Qualifications:
- No experience necessary, but must be comfortable with technology and willing to learn.
- Complete a background check

Job Description: Slides Tech
Opportunity: Slides Tech
Summary:
The Worship Arts ministry at Kaleo is actively engaged in preparing hearts for worship through song. Every team member plays an active role in leading others in worship and removing obstacles. The A/V tech team plays a vital role in making the worship team heard, visual aids seen, and our Sunday service available online. The Slides Technician sets up Kaleo’s prepared slides on Sunday mornings and runs them as the service goes along.
Time Requirements:
- Sundays, 10:45am-1:30pm, at least once every two months
This Position Reports to:
Worship Arts Director, Mary Giles mgiles@kaleoalaska.org
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Log into Kaleo’s Proclaim account
- Double check the order of worship and songs are correct
- Work with the teaching pastor to make sure slides or videos are accurate
- During the service, anticipate slide changes and troubleshoot any issues
Qualifications:
- No experience necessary, but must be comfortable with technology and willing to learn.
- Complete a background check

Job Description: Kaleo Kids Sunday School Teacher
Opportunity: Kaleo Kids Sunday School Teacher
Summary:
Kaleo Kids is a thriving ministry for children centered around the good news of Jesus. Kids learn that God made them, He is the rescuer from sin and death, and He will make all things new. Curriculum comes from the Gospel Project. Every lesson comes back to a Christ centered connection. We see Jesus from Genesis to Revelation. Kids play games, do crafts and activities, laugh and have fun together.
Time Requirements:
10:45 a.m. – 1:30 p.m. One Sunday a month for nursery, toddlers, and PreK. Two Sunday’s every 6 weeks on a rotational schedule for K-5 th classes.
This Position Reports to:
Children’s Ministry Director, Lucy Matthew, lmatthew@kaleoalaska.org.
Responsibilities and Authority:
- Accountable for teaching Biblical truth and keeping the class on track.
- Utilize assistant teachers and teen helpers effectively.
- Check-in with your assistant and helpers Saturday night to confirm for Sunday morning.
- Attend 10:45 a.m. devotion/communion on Sunday Mornings that you are scheduled to serve, led by Pastor Chris for church wide ministry team of volunteers for that Sunday.
- Access Ministry Grid for Online lesson plans and dates.
- Accept scheduling confirmation on Planning Center for Kaleo Church.
- Attend quarterly children’s meeting for one hour after church, (4 times a year.)
Qualifications:
- Member of Kaleo Church
- Enjoy working with and caring for children (Nursery – 5 th )
- Pass basic background check through Department of Justice, paid for by Kaleo.
Ready to Apply?

All your children shall be taught by the Lord, and great shall be the peace of your children.
~Isaiah 54:13
Children are precious to God and to us here at Kaleo. What children choose to believe about God is the most important thing about them. Our goal is to partner with parents to teach children who God is and what He has done. We pray that children would put their faith and hope in Jesus, so that they would have true joy and peace.

Children's Director
Lucy has lived in Alaska since elementary school. She graduated from San Diego Christian College in 2004 with a degree in Bible and Psychology. She is married to Patrick and they have 4 children.
She has a background in administrative work, youth ministry, basketball, and running. Her crazy dream is to be a farmer and she loves all things old-school country.
She is thankful for life in Jesus and her family. God's power in creation and redemption will never cease to amaze her.

Church Membership
Are you ready to take the next step? Are you ready to call Kaleo, home? Church membership is an essential part of a thriving church. Church membership means you are “in”, you are vested and you are proclaiming that you are part of this local body of believers. We believe in church membership and we take church membership seriously. Because of that, there are a few steps involved.
This process is applicable to those 18+. (Children of members will be considered members until they become adults and will have to enter this process to become adult members).
The steps below don’t have to happen in this order, but they are all required to become a member:
1. Agree with our “Statement of Faith” and “Statement of Beliefs” found in our Membership Covenant. Download the PDF of our Membership Covenant .
2. Get Baptized (if you haven't already). We have regular baptisms, so if you still need to do this, please Signup to be Baptized to be part of the next one.
3. Attend our Membership Class. Membership classes are held 2-3 times a year. They are free to attend and dinner and childcare are provided. Watch for a signup on our home page. Listen to the 6-Part series, "This is Kaleo Church" to learn more about what we believe.
4. Fill out the Membership Application to let us know you’d like to proceed with membership. This helps us get to know you before the elder interview. If you are married, we ask that each spouse fill out their own application separately.
5. Final step: Have a membership interview with a(n) elder(s). This is more of a conversation than an interview, where an elder will want to hear your story and how you were saved. They will typically go over the membership covenant and ask if you have any questions. Once you've completed the above steps and applied, an elder will reach out to you to schedule this.
Once the above items are complete, the elders will review all the membership applicants and either approve or ask for more info from an applicant before approving. Once the elders have approved, we would like to bring the applicants up on stage and introduce them as a member of Kaleo at our next member installation.

Men's Ministry
Purpose:
Men's ministry at Kaleo sets out to accomplish two goals:
1) Call up men into Godly disciples of Jesus, and 2) bond men closer to each other in the church through serving and learning together.
Iron sharpens iron,
and one man sharpens another.
- Proverbs 27:17
Kaleo men gather together on the first Monday of each month to eat, have fun, and intentionally study scripture and how it applies to their own lives.
How to Join:
1. To see the upcoming schedule and sign-up,
Follow this link to the sign-up page
2. For regular updates and to communicate with others in the group,
Download the Church Center App.
3. Join us on the next "First Monday" of the month from 6-8PM at the church building.
1301 W. 100th Ave. Anchorage, AK 99515
Frontline Men:
Kaleo Frontline Men are ready to serve if you have found yourself in need of light home or auto maintenance. Some of our men have volunteered to invest an hour or two a month into helping our community. If you have a need, please take a minute to fill out THIS FORM. If you are part of the Kaleo Men's Ministry and would like to serve in this way, please APPLY HERE to join Frontline Men.
Resources:
Men's Ministry Resouces are meant to help you go deeper in your walk with The Lord. Some of these resources will be explored and referenced together in our gatherings and some are meant for you to explore on your own.
Go to the Men's Ministry Resources Page

Our Story
GOD is awesome and orchestrated the birth of Kaleo! God had placed a desire in the heart of Pastor Brad Sutter at Cornerstone Church in Anchorage Alaska to one day plant a church. Years later Pastor Brad met Chris Cha, who was on track to start a church. However, Chris didn't want to just go out and start a church on his own. It was really important to Chris to be sent out by a local body.
What a perfect fit! Cornerstone church brought Chris on staff to Pastor their Life Groups with the plan to send Him out to plant a church in the future. Cornerstone had such a heart for sending out a Church to the glory of God that they told Chris to "fish from our waters" and to take anyone who felt led to go with him.
In 2015, Kaleo Church was launched intentionally, with great love and purpose by Cornerstone Church. There were many families who joined the mission of Kaleo. We are so blessed to have been so supported by a parent church. Not only did they give us support financially but they also gave us accountability to their Elder board until we were able to have our own in place. It was such a cool day to have Pastor Brad come and install the elders at Kaleo about a year later... such a sweet time.
God has blessed Cornerstone and great things are going on for the Kingdom through them and throughout other churches in the City of Anchorage. God is also using and growing Kaleo for His purposes and we are so thankful to be a part of His work in the City. We hope to also be a Church that God uses to send out and plant more churches for His glory.
May Jesus be lifted high and made known!
Why Church Planting?
A vigorous and continuous approach to church planting is the only way to guarantee an increase in the number of believers, and is one of the best ways to renew the whole body of Christ.

Plan Your Visit
Visit Kaleo this Sunday at 11:45 am!
Whether you're exploring your faith for the first time, are new to the Christian faith, or have been a Jesus follower for years, we invite you to join us at Kaleo Church in Anchorage, Alaska. We offer a single service on Sundays at 11:45 am.
What to expect
Our service include family worship, where all ages are invited to join in our call to worship activities through prayer and song. Prior to the sermon, we dismiss our children from Nursery through 5th grade to enjoy Kaleo Kids Ministry.
Our sermon messages are brought by a team of elders and teachers led by our Pastor Chris Cha .
Kaleo is a casual, authentic environment. You can wear jeans and a t-shirt or your Sunday best. Most of us dress for comfort. We want you to feel comfortable, too. Kaleo invites you to come as you are.
Location & MAP
Kaleo Church shares a building with Kingdom Life Church, and is located at 1301 W. 100th Ave in Anchorage.

Prayer
"Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing praise. Is anyone among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord. And the prayer of faith will save the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. And if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven. Therefore, confess your sins to one another and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person has great power as it is working." ~James 5:13-16
If you would like prayer, please let us know on the Connect Card , and we will forward prayer requests to our elders for prayer. Please let us know if you'd like us to contact you about a request .
Grace and Peace.

Re|Engage
Whether your marriage needs to be reignited or completely resurrected, Re|Engage is a safe place for you and your spouse to reconnect.
Would you rate your marriage as a 2 or a 10?
Nothing worthwhile comes easily, and Christian marriage is no exception. A happy and successful marriage—one worthy of God's design—requires a good deal of work.
Whether your marriage is a solid “8” and you want to get to a “9” or you’re stuck on “2” and wondering if you’re going to make it through another year . . . Re|Engage is for you!
Re|Engage offers hope to marriages by helping couples move toward oneness through stories of grace and teaching, focusing on things like humility, confession, forgiveness, and commitment. Re|Engage uses a small-group approach to facilitate marital health, and rests on the belief that God’s Word, God’s Spirit, and God’s people offer everything a marriage needs to get well. Unlike traditional counseling or mentoring in which couples meet with an individual or even another couple, we believe life and marriage transformation best occurs within relationships with others – community.
Re|Engage meets in-person on Wednesdays during our curriculum periods in the Fall and Winter , featuring large-group worship and powerful teaching or testimony, followed by life-changing small-group times. Re|Engage is an 16-week program and costs $150 per couple. The fee covers the cost of two workbooks per couple and participation in a closed group with four or five other couples. These groups will be led by a “facilitator couple,” who have already been through Re|Engage.
Ready to go?
Click HERE to register.
"Wait, I have questions."
We thought you might. Below are some FAQ. Please contact us if you have more questions. Re|Engage is the best! You guys will be so happy you chose to do it!
What is the Re|Engage Marriage Study?
This marriage enhancement and restoration course seeks to:
- REVIVE a marriage that has gone “cold”
- RESTORE a broken marriage relationship
- REPLACE damaging words and behaviors with relationally healthy ones
- REPLENISH marriages with a new spirit of God’s design for couples in a way that is sustainable and benefits our children, family, friends, co-workers and community.
Each weekly study includes a video or live testimony and marriage-specific discussions. We convene as a large group initially, then disburse into established smaller groups of 3 – 5 married couples led by a trained, married facilitator couple where important, biblical topics are covered, such as: Love, Grace, Humility, Forgiveness, Understanding, Communication, Emotional Intimacy, and others.
When is Re|Engage offered?
Re|Engage is offered every Fall-Winter. Meetings are held one weeknight per week for 16 weeks, and each session lasts about 2 hours.
Where are the weekly sessions held?
1301 W 100th Ave. Anchorage, AK 99515 (Kingdom Life Church Building)
Why is the Re|engage marriage program needed?
Financial or job stress, unmet expectations, family issues, and social pressures can unravel the bonds of mutual love and respect God created to exist between husbands and wives. New couples to Re|engage often describe their marriage experience as unloving, disappointing, unhealthy, disrespectful, broken, disconnected, passionless, inconsistent, suffering, or miserable. Some remain married only for the sake of children, tax benefits, or public perception. Others coexist more like roommates than husband and wife. Some are separated, and others are married and divorced a few times. Not surprisingly, men and women (and their children) suffer mental strife, emotional trauma, financial loss, substance abuse, serious health problems, infidelity, separation, or divorce – none of which were intended by God for men and women in marriage. Re|engage was developed to restore husbands and wives to each other in meaningful and sustainable ways within the covenant of God’s love and purpose, ultimately reflecting a healthy, happy marriage to our children and all those we know in our daily lives.
Is Re|engage traditional marriage therapy?
No. Re|engage is NOT marriage counseling or therapy.
- The course follows an established, topical format accompanied by a participant study guide. The program is only offered through a network of selected churches and has proven itself effective nationwide with thousands of married couples. Kaleo Church in Anchorage is an approved provider of the program in the area.
- The trained facilitator couples leading your small group studies are not licensed marriage therapists. Re|engage facilitator couples are committed, married couples who share a passion for helping other married couples through this particular study and are dedicated to serving God within our church in the area of marriage ministry.
- Married couples who complete the program in the right spirit and mindset can expect to see renewed levels of mutual understanding, communication, love, joy, passion, peace and intimacy which can only be attributed to Christ’s restorative work in our relationships.
Is child care available?
Yes! We will have childcare available for ages 0 to 5 th grade. Registration for childcare will be available during our registration period.
What is the cost to attend?
Cost is $150 per couple.
Is the program available only to married couples?
Yes. Re|engage is designed specifically for traditional married couples of any tenure, and the topics covered are relevant to marriage at any stage.
Can non-christian married couples attend Re|Engage?
Yes! Re|engage is a marriage program built around biblical principles that restore and reconnect marriage relationships, but these course topics covered in Re|engage but can benefit non-Christian marriages too.
Are we required to attend every evening?
We understand that one or both spouses may not be able to attend all 16 sessions. As a marriage program, however, optimal results are attained by jointly attending as many weekly sessions as possible.
Is my privacy protected in the group?
Absolutely. Re|engage leadership and facilitator couples adhere to a strict confidentiality policy among ourselves and all attendees. What’s discussed in the group stays in the group, and participants are assigned to the same group for the duration of the program. Strict adherence to a non-disclosure, “no-gossip” policy ensures you can participate freely and honestly without worry.
What about physical abuse, chronic depression or anxiety disorders, substance abuse, or suicide issues? Can re|engage help marriages with these problems?
No. Serious conditions like these are outside the scope of the Re|engage marriage program. Our church staff may be able to assist Kaleo Church members with pastoral counseling or a referral, but whether Kaleo is your church or not, it would be in yours and your spouse’s best interest to seek professional or medical help for these problems before participating in a marriage-specific program.
When and how do I sign up for Re|Engage?
Enrollment for the Re|engage series begins every August for the Fall-Winter session. Once registration opens you can sign up at www.kaleoalaska.org/reengage .
Who do I contact for more information about the Re|Engage marriage program?
Please contact pastor Chris Cha at reengage@kaleoalaska.org
For more information on re|engage please go to: https://www.reengage.org

Grow
How to GROW?
1. Confess Jesus as Lord believing that he died on the cross to pay the penalty for your sin, and that he rose from the dead to prove it. Ask Jesus to forgive you for your sin, save you, and take you to heaven when you die. This is salvation. Once you have it, you cannot lose it. God Himself keeps you.
2. Get Baptized to show that you have decided to follow Jesus, as your first act of obedience to Him.
3.Pray. Read the Bible. Ask questions.
4. Join a K-Group. It's a community of friends to fellowship and learn together.
5. See our resources tab to the right to find rich and robust content for your edification and spiritual development.
6. Become a member of a local Bible believing church. We would love you to join us here at Kaleo! However, if we aren't a good fit, we understand everyones needs are differnt and we can help you find a churh that is a good fit.
Questions? Please email info@kaleoalaska.org

Serving at Kaleo
Did you know God has already planned for you to make an impact?
“For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them." (Ephesians 2:10)
Maybe you’re ready to start simply – and simply start! Or maybe you’re looking for a better fit or more impactful opportunity. Either way, you’re in the right place.
Below you will find several opportunities to serve in the areas where we have current needs. If you are looking to serve in a way not listed, please reach out to us at the email address at the bottom of this page.
All postions are categorized by their ministry area. Click any of the available positions to open its job description and learn more.

SK Son
Elder
SK has lived in Alaska since 1987. He is married to Meehee and they have one son. He loves his family dearly. He is self employed, and likes to play golf and guitar. He is thankful for Jesus and came to faith when he was 26 years old. God's forgiveness and grace moves his heart.

K-Groups
K-Groups
The "K" in K-Groups stands for koinonia which means "communion" or "fellowship" in greek. K-Groups are one of the primary ways people of Kaleo connect with one another.
To See what K-Groups are available, click the button below.
We encourage you to explore a few different k-groups before you commit to one so that you can connect with our community.
Interested in leading a k-group?
We are always on the lookout for more K-Group Leaders. If you are interested in exploring that idea, we would love to chat. For more details or to start that discussion, check out our K-Group Leader Job Description.

What We Believe
We hope you find your time with us to be meaningful, informative and beneficial. If you have questions our pastors and elders are available. You can also find this information in our Membership Covenant .
Every religion in the world says that we have to "do something" or "be something" in order to earn God's approval and forgiveness, except for Christianity. The Bible says that, while we were still sinners God loved us and sent His Son to die for us in order to pay the debt that we owed for our sin. God Himself came to rescue us! ( Romans 5:8 , Genesis 1:26 , John 14:9 , Colossians 1:15-23 , Hebrews 1:1-3 )
The Bible says that we are all separated from God and in need of a Savior, because of sin. “No one is righteous, no, not one… for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God… and the penalty for sin is death... and we are dead in our sin." ( Romans 3:10 and Romans 6:23 , Ephesians 2:1-3 )
If we are dead, there is nothing we can do. We need someone else to make us alive again, someone to rescue us. Good news...
"...But the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord…" ( Romans 6:23)
"...But God, being rich in mercy, because of the great love with which he loved us, even when we were dead in our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ—by grace you have been saved... through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works, so that no one may boast. For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them." ( Ephesians 2:4-10 )
A true gift can not be earned. It must simly be accepted. Only those who accept God's free gift of forgiveness will receive it. The good things we do can never undo the bad that we have done. The good things we do can never undo the curse that is and has been on the earth since Adam and Eve decided to trust in themselves and not in God. See (Genesis 1-3)
GOOD NEWS! It doesn't matter what you have done. You are not too "bad" for God to save. Though our sins are as scarlet, He will wash us white as snow. Jesus is the only one who can. It is the Father's great pleasure because he loves us.
We are all alone before God and we are accountable to Him for our life. The Bible says that "if we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from ALL unrighteousness." ( John 1:9 ) Confess means to agree with God that we are sinners and acknowledge our need for a Savior.
God also assures us that once we are His, no one can change that or take us away from Him. He has redeemed us forever. He keeps us. We are sealed and secure by His Spirit. ( John 6:35-40 , John 10:28-30, Ephesians 1:13 )
What a GOOD Father! God loves His children and has come to rescue them. He did not leave us all alone. He came to be with us... to sacrifice Himself for us so that we might one day live again forever with Him. He came to save us, to bring us back to Him... ( Genesis 1:26 , John 14:9 , Colossians 1:15-23 , Hebrews 1:1-3 )
The Bible says that if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved. For with the heart one believes and is justified, and with the mouth one confesses and is saved… For everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved… For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life… And there is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given among men by which we must be saved. ( Romans 10:9-10 , Romans 10:13 , John 3:16 , Acts 4:12 )
Not only will He save us individually from our sin, but His death and ressurection also will rescue and renew the entire Creation one day! And on that day, finally there will be no more suffering, death, tears, pain, sadness, and brokenness. Everything will be made right. We will do life on the new earth ( Revelation 21:1 ) without the constraints of the curse of sin and death. We will live forever with God, which is how he always intened for things to be. Humans were never meant to die... The earth was not meant to degenerate and decay... God did not make things that way origianlly. The curse will be broken. All will be restored.
We cannot even imagine what God has prepared on that day for those who love Him! What a glorious day that will be! Thank you Jesus! ( 1 Corinthians 2:9 , Romans 8:22 , Revelation 21:4-5 , Revelation 22:1-5 , John 17:3 )
